behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction explicitly names: go.mod with v2.24.11, minifiers/config.go with KeepNamespaces []string{"", "x-bind"}, minifiers/minifiers_test.go with #14669, x-bind:href="myicon", :href="myicon", and the commands go test ./minifiers/..., go build ./, go vet ./minifiers/.... All asserted paths, strings, and values in tests are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: update to v2.24.11, configure KeepNamespaces with []string{"", "x-bind"}, and add specific test cases with exact strings. It tells the agent precisely WHAT to change and HOW, leaving no reasoning about the solution required.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_config_has_keep_namespaces asserts the exact string 'KeepNamespaces: []string{"", "x-bind"}' in config.go. An alternative correct fix (e.g., different formatting, variable assignment, or using a helper) would fail this text-matching test. Similarly, test_go_mod_dependencies_updated checks for the exact string 'github.com/tdewolff/minify/v2 v2.24.11'.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_go_mod_dependencies_updated, test_config_has_keep_namespaces, test_minifiers_test_has_alpine_cases) are purely text-matching with 'assert X in open(path).read()'. While pass-to-pass tests do execute code (go test, go build, go vet), the core f2p tests only check file contents, not behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only check that specific strings exist in files. An agent could simply append the required strings as comments to the files (e.g., add '// KeepNamespaces: []string{"", "x-bind"}' as a comment) without actually implementing the fix. The go test pass-to-pass test would catch this only if the existing tests break, but the f2p tests themselves are trivially satisfiable without a real fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four requirements from the instruction are tested: dependency update (test_go_mod_dependencies_updated), SVG config (test_config_has_keep_namespaces), regression tests (test_minifiers_test_has_alpine_cases), and existing tests passing (test_repo_minifiers_test, test_repo_go_build, test_repo_go_vet).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names, have docstrings explaining whether they are fail-to-pass or pass-to-pass, and are logically grouped by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names across the instruction and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact file (minifiers/config.go), the exact option (KeepNamespaces), the exact value ([]string{"", "x-bind"}), the exact dependency version (v2.24.11), and the exact test file (minifiers/minifiers_test.go). This is far beyond describing what's broken — it prescribes the exact implementation.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized edits to go.mod, minifiers/config.go (adding one field), and minifiers/minifiers_test.go (adding test cases). No full-file rewrites or contradictions.
